tools.name: Impacket, PowerShell script, Drokbk.exe (.NET implant), Soldier (custom malware variant)

ttps.technique: Use of publicly disclosed POCs for initial access and persistence
Use of Impacket to move laterally through a compromised organization
Use of PowerShell scripts to enumerate admin accounts and enable RDP connections

summary: A subgroup of the Iranian nation-state actor Mint Sandstorm has refined its tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) to attack high-value targets in various sectors. This subgroup rapidly weaponizes N-day vulnerabilities and conducts highly-targeted phishing campaigns to access environments of interest. Microsoft is publishing details on known tradecraft alongside corresponding detections and mitigations to help organizations protect against this threat.
